articles.title: Nothing’s modular CMF Headphone Pro are down to their lowest price to date
articles.description:
Over the past several years, Nothing’s budget-centric subsidiary, CMF, has built a reputation for producing attractive, inexpensive products with features often reserved for gadgets that cost twice the price. The CMF Headphone Pro — the company’s first pair of over-ear wireless headphones — fit precisely in that lane, especially now that they’re on sale at Amazon in both light gray and a subtle green shade for an all-time low of $69 ($30 off).
CMF Headphone Pro

Where to Buy:

 $99 $69 at Amazon (light gray)
 $99 $69 at Amazon (green)

Although the Headphone Pro eschew the retro-transparent design found on Nothing wares like the Headphone 1, I wouldn’t necessarily say that’s a bad thing given the polarizing, squared-off design of the latter. Instead, the Pro go all in on the curves, with circular earcups, cushions, and a plush headband. You can also add an additional pop of color with CMF’s $25 interchangeable cushions, which, as of now, are available in either orange or the aforementioned green hue.
Unlike many premium pairs of noise-canceling headphones, the Pro ditch touch-capacitive controls for physical buttons — a plus, as far as I’m concerned — allowing you to control playback, adjust the volume, and tweak a range of other settings using a customizable action button and a multi-function roller. Nothing also includes something called an Energy Slider, so you can adjust the treble and bass balance on the fly without digging into Nothing’s app to make EQ adjustments.
The biggest draw of the Headphone Pro, aside from their adaptive ANC, is that they offer a whopping 100 hours of battery life per charge, ensuring you can travel just about anywhere without having to top them off. That number drops to 50 hours with active noise cancellation enabled; however, that’s still 10 hours more than what you’d get with a higher-end pair like Sony’s WH-1000XM6 and 20 hours more than the latest Bose QuietComfort Ultra Headphones. The fact that they support Google Fast Pair and the higher-quality LDAC codec is just a plus.
Other alternatives to consider
Beats Solo 4
With lossless USB-C audio, 50 hours of battery life, Find My support, spatial audio, and comfy ear cups, the Beats Solo 4 offer significant quality-of-life improvements over their predecessors. They still deliver that bass-rich Beats sound, too. Read our review.

Where to Buy:

 $199.95 $129.95 at Amazon
 $199.95 $129 at Walmart
 $199.95 $149.99 at Best Buy


Sony WH-CH520
The Sony WH-CH520 offer a lot for very little. The on-ear Bluetooth headphones provide 50 hours of battery life and support hands-free voice assistants, including Google Assistant and Siri. They also offer multipoint Bluetooth support and can tap into Google’s Find Hub network.

Where to Buy:

 $59.99 $38 at Amazon 
 $59.99 $38 at Best Buy
 $59.99 $39.99 at Target

articles.description: The process of making 30 billion tons of concrete every year produces more carbon pollution than all the world's ships and planes put together. David Pogue reports.

articles.title: The Stars My Destination is classic proto-cyberpunk
articles.description:
Gully Foyle is my name… | Image: Brick Tower Press	

This might feel like a somewhat obvious recommendation to some, but it flew under my radar until now. Alfred Bester's The Stars My Destination (originally published as Tiger! Tiger! in the UK) is a 1956 sci-fi novel that some have cited as a precursor to cyberpunk. It's a work I admit I have some conflicted feelings about, but one I think is well worth reading if you consider yourself a fan of sci-fi. It's also well worth seeking out a physical copy, something I wish I had known before I started reading it in the objectively inferior e-book form that can't capture the ergodic elements of the climax.
It's hard to explain the plot of The Star …
Read the full story at The Verge.

articles.title: Marathon battery life makes Keychron’s Ultra 8K keyboards its best yet
articles.description:
The Keychron Q1 Ultra 8K in white (left) and the V5 Ultra 8K in black (right). | Photo: Antonio G. Di Benedetto / The Verge	

Keychron's expansive keyboard catalog covers everything from basic full-size mechanical boards to niche layouts like southpaw Alice or a one-handed half-keyboard for gaming. Its Q and V series are among our go-to recommendations for off-the-shelf keyboards, and the latest iterations - the Q and V Ultra models - are a straightforward upgrade to the lineup, with benefits for regular users and deep-in-the-weeds keyboard nerds alike.
I've been testing two Ultra keyboards: the Keychron V5 Ultra 8K and Keychron Q1 Ultra 8K. The $119.99 V5 Ultra is a near-full-size 1800-layout board with a number pad in a plastic case, while the $229.99 Q1 Ultra i …
Read the full story at The Verge.

articles.title: The future of local TV news has taken a Trumpian turn
articles.description:
This is The Stepback, a weekly newsletter breaking down one essential story from the tech world. For more stories on Big Tech versus politics in Washington, DC, follow Tina Nguyen and read Regulator. The Stepback arrives in our subscribers' inboxes at 8AM ET. Opt in for The Stepback here.
How it started
A long time ago, in 2004, the Federal Communications Commission laid down a rule designed to prevent a monopoly: No one company could broadcast to more than 39 percent of all the TV households in the United States. But then Donald Trump returned to the White House in 2025. Brendan Carr became FCC chairman and immediately kicked off a deregu …
Read the full story at The Verge.

articles.title: Defence stocks breakout: Should you book profits or buy the dip?
articles.description: Defence stocks are rallying sharply with strong technical breakouts, though select heavyweights signal near-term overbought conditions. Analysts advise avoiding chasing the surge and instead adopting a buy-on-dips strategy. Broader market momentum remains positive, with selective opportunities in smallcaps and identified stocks showing potential upside.

articles.title: Australia's most-decorated soldier vows to fight war crime charges
articles.description: Ben Roberts-Smith has given his first statement since he was charged with five counts of the war crime of murder last week.

articles.title: Humanoid robot sprints to victory in Beijing, beating human half-marathon record
articles.description: A humanoid robot has won a half-marathon race for robots in Beijing, running faster than the human record

articles.title: Sensex, Nifty to extend gains on Monday? 5 factors to guide D-St
articles.description: Indian stock markets are poised for a strong opening, extending Friday's gains as GIFT Nifty surged. Optimism surrounding a potential resolution to the Iran-US conflict and falling oil prices are boosting sentiment. Major bank earnings and a strengthening rupee also provide support, with FIIs showing consistent buying interest.

topStories.title: Netflix documentary director on Hulk Hogan's Trump support and the backlash that followed him to the end
topStories.description: Hulk Hogan completed 25 hours of interviews for a Netflix documentary before his death at 71, and the director says he aimed to show the full person.

topStories.title: Open: This is "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan," April 19, 2026
topStories.description: This week on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan," U.S. Ambassador Mike Waltz joins as President Trump says negotiators are headed to Pakistan for a peace deal with Iran. Plus. Former White House adviser on energy security Amos Hochstein and former U.S. Attorney General Eric Holder join.

topStories.title: Trump tells ABC News peace agreement for Iran 'will happen one way or another'
topStories.description: President Donald Trump spoke on Sunday to ABC News' Jonathan Karl.

topStories.title: Cleanup underway in Midwest after tornadoes leave path of damage, destruction
topStories.description: Communities across the Midwest were starting the cleanup process Saturday after multiple tornadoes touched down and severe weather struck areas from the Great Lakes to Texas.

topStories.title: Nature: Prairie chickens in South Dakota
topStories.description: We leave you this Sunday morning with prairie chickens and sharp tail grouse near Ft. Pierre, South Dakota. Videographer: Kevin Kjergaard.

topStories.title: Cementing a more sustainable alternative to concrete
topStories.description: The process of making 30 billion tons of concrete every year produces more carbon pollution than all the world's ships and planes put together. Now, the North Carolina and Denmark-based Biomason is using biotechnology, including naturally-occurring microbes, to create "biocement" that's just as sturdy but emits much less CO2. David Pogue reports.

topStories.title: Hormuz at Standstill, Denting US-Iran Peace Deal Hopes
topStories.description: Shipping in the Strait of Hormuz was at a near standstill early Sunday after Iran reversed its decision to reopen the waterway and fired on vessels attempting to pass, warning it would block transits as long as the US blockade of Iranian ports remained.

topStories.title: Jake Cronenworth takes 96 mph fastball to the face, stays in the game as Padres rally to beat Angels
topStories.description: Jake Cronenworth was hit in the face by a 96 mph fastball during the Padres' win over the Angels and stayed in the game, drawing praise from his manager.

topStories.title: Susan Lucci says a Playboy magazine tipped her off she lost coveted Bond girl role
topStories.description: Susan Lucci says she lost the Bond girl role to this Hollywood actress, realizing it when she spotted a Playboy cover in the makeup room of "All My Children."

topStories.title: Amazon, USPS and the risk of a widening delivery divide in rural America
topStories.description: The new deal between Amazon and the U.S. Postal Service has many implications, maybe none greater than what it means for package delivery to rural Americans.

topStories.title: What to know about US-Iran standoff over the Strait of Hormuz
topStories.description: Trump accuses Iran of violating the ceasefire as he announces plans to send US negotiators to Pakistan for more talks.

topStories.description: At least three students were injured in a shooting near the University of Iowa early Sunday morning, according to police

dailyNews.description: More credit card issuers now approve applicants without a Social Security number, giving scammers new ways to commit identity theft using stolen data.

dailyNews.title: Pakistan Gears Up For Crucial US-Iran Talks Amid Tight Security
dailyNews.description: Pakistan deploys 10,000 police and readies the Serena Hotel for a second round of high-stakes US-Iran peace talks, as Islamabad pushes for a lasting deal in West Asia.

dailyNews.title: 'Lee Cronin's The Mummy' Review: A gruesome reimagining of classic Hollywood monster
dailyNews.description: Fox News Digital reviews "Lee Cronin's The Mummy" starring Jack Reynor, Laia Costa, Veronica Falcón, May Calamawy and Natalie Grace. Written/directed by Lee Cronin.

dailyNews.title: Cold case breakthrough solves teen killing after suspect lived free for decades: 'Better be afraid'
dailyNews.description: Michigan State Police say DNA technology solved the 1983 cold case murder of 16-year-old Sheri Jo Elliott, identifying Roni Collins as the killer.

dailyNews.title: Gas prices may not drop below $3 a gallon until next year: Energy Secretary Wright
dailyNews.description: Gas prices are more than $4 per gallon on average in the U.S. right now, a massive spike from just months ago.

dailyNews.title: Amb. Mike Waltz says Iran doesn't 'have the cards,' will seek new deal: Full interview
dailyNews.description: U.S. Ambassador to the United Nations Mike Waltz tells Meet the Press that “everything's on the table” and President Trump “is prepared to escalate to de-escalate” in Iran.

dailyNews.title: Using the ocean to power data centers
dailyNews.description: The renewable energy company Panthalassa says it has a solution to the proliferation of AI data centers, which consume massive amounts of energy and are the cause of increased carbon pollution: sea-based data centers, powered by wave energy.

dailyNews.title: Gulf Equities End Mixed Following Trump's 'Serious Violation' Claim And Hormuz Standoff
dailyNews.description: Gulf markets trade lower as Saudi Aramco slips 0.7% amid fresh uncertainty over the Strait of Hormuz. Iran's move to shut the waterway rattles regional investor sentiment.

dailyNews.title: Iranian President Pezeshkian Rebukes Trump Over Nuclear Policy
dailyNews.description: Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian challenges Donald Trump's authority to curb Iran's nuclear rights, calling U.S. restrictions "unjustified" as regional tensions soar.

dailyNews.title: Tour New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani’s office
dailyNews.description: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani (D) walks Meet the Press through his office and shares the advice he’s received from past mayors.

dailyNews.description: A look at the features for this week's broadcast of the Emmy-winning program, hosted by Jane Pauley.

dailyNews.description: Meet the Press Moderator Kristen Welker joins Sunday TODAY’s Willie Geist to share new NBC News polling that shows 67% of Americans disapprove of how President Donald Trump is handling the Iran war.

topStories.title: The future of local TV news has taken a Trumpian turn
topStories.description:
This is The Stepback, a weekly newsletter breaking down one essential story from the tech world. For more stories on Big Tech versus politics in Washington, DC, follow Tina Nguyen and read Regulator. The Stepback arrives in our subscribers' inboxes at 8AM ET. Opt in for The Stepback here.
How it started
A long time ago, in 2004, the Federal Communications Commission laid down a rule designed to prevent a monopoly: No one company could broadcast to more than 39 percent of all the TV households in the United States. But then Donald Trump returned to the White House in 2025. Brendan Carr became FCC chairman and immediately kicked off a deregu …
Read the full story at The Verge.

dailyNews.title: A renewed threat to JPL as the Trump administration tries again to cut NASA
dailyNews.description: The cuts, proposed in the Trump administration's 2027 budget request to Congress, would pose further challenges to the venerable La Cañada Flintridge facility and its space missions.

dailyNews.title: Ordered free, still locked up: Judges fume as Trump administration holds ICE detainees
dailyNews.description: A legal maneuver once reserved for death row inmates and suspected terrorists has become the only recourse for immigrant detainees, who have flooded federal courts in California with thousands of petitions for freedom.

dailyNews.title: Syrian Billionaires Needed a Favor in Washington. They Invoked the Trump Name.
dailyNews.description: The attempt by the Khayyats to influence foreign policy while discussions are underway about potential Trump family deals is an increasingly common feature of the president’s second term.

dailyNews.title: We analyzed Trump’s public remarks. His White House ballroom is a top priority.
dailyNews.description: The president has publicly highlighted his White House ballroom project on roughly a third of the days this year, a Washington Post analysis found.

dailyNews.title: In Angola, love for an American pope but not for an American president
dailyNews.description: In Luanda, Catholics expressed love and admiration for Pope Leo XIV, the first American pope, but not so much for President Trump because of the war in Iran.

dailyNews.title: Pope says 'tyrants' speech was not aimed at Trump
dailyNews.description: The pontiff says his remarks have been misinterpreted after a spat with the US president.

dailyNews.title: U.S. Installs a Trump Loyalist to Lead ‘Grand Conspiracy’ Case Into Trump Foes
dailyNews.description: A former lawyer for President Trump’s campaign, Joseph diGenova, is said to be planning to split time between Miami and Fort Pierce, where a grand jury overseen by a Trump-favored judge sits.

dailyNews.title: They’re Young. They’re Conservative. And They’re Split Over the War.
dailyNews.description: On college campuses from the Northeast to the Southwest, the conflict in Iran is testing the fealty of young Republicans during the second Trump administration.

dailyNews.title: Potential 2028 Democrats Audition in Michigan,With a Focus on Trump
dailyNews.description: Former Vice President Kamala Harris, Senator Cory Booker of New Jersey and Gov. Andy Beshear of Kentucky spoke at a gathering of party insiders in Detroit, fueling presidential speculation.

dailyNews.title: With Trump Novices, Can the U.S. Win the ‘Art Olympics’?
dailyNews.description: After the State Department overhauled the process for choosing an artist for the Venice Biennale, it gave control to a woman who previously owned a pet food store.

dailyNews.title: Trump says U.S. officials are traveling to Pakistan for new Iran talks
dailyNews.description: Sources said Vice President Vance, special envoy Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner would attend the talks.
